ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general barley beverage class; no specific lemon-flavoured less-sugar packaged variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJL covers barley-based beverages broadly; lemon-flavoured less-sugar packaged variant is not individually coded. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged lemon barley beverage; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve portion is one 250 ml can or bottle, providing approximately 75 kcal and 17.75 g of sugar from this less-sugar packaged lemon barley drink.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Barley (Less Sugar, Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D05010028 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sugar-sweetened beverages and lemon-flavoured drinks analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific packaged lemon barley less-sugar product. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugar-sweetened soft drinks and lemon-flavoured beverages with comparable sugar content (~7g/100g). GL per 250g serving calculated as (65 × 7.1 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.5g. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary by formulation.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage content (7.0g sugar/100g) exceeding the 5g/100g SSB threshold; independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines
hypertension.guidance: Despite the 'less sugar' label, this beverage contains 7.0g sugar/100g and should be avoided by individuals with hypertension; choose genuinely sugar-free barley water or plain water to eliminate SSB-related blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.0g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugars cause rapid glycaemic spikes and the ADA SSB hard gate is triggered
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely; the 7.0g sugar/100g with zero fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation, and even 'less sugar' variants remain clinically unsuitable — opt for unsweetened home-brewed barley water without added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk through triglyceride elevation, insulin resistance, and inflammatory pathways
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this packaged lemon barley drink due to its 7.0g sugar/100g content; the low sodium (7mg/100g) is not sufficient to offset the cardiovascular risk from habitual SSB consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (7.0g/100g) with zero fibre and no anti-inflammatory nutrients promotes pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened packaged beverage; unsweetened barley water may offer modest beta-glucan benefit but the added sugar in this product promotes inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Barley Drink (Less Sugar, Packaged) is a commercially manufactured, ready-to-drink beverage popular in Singapore and across Chinese-influenced Southeast Asian communities. It is produced by extracting barley grain in water, adding lemon flavouring, and incorporating a reduced quantity of sugar compared to standard barley drinks. The result is a lightly sweet, mildly tart, pale-yellow liquid with a subtle grain undertone and refreshing citrus note. The texture is thin and watery, similar to diluted fruit juice. Per 100g, it provides 30 kcal, 7.1g carbohydrate (of which 7.0g is sugar), 0g protein, 0g fat, 0g dietary fibre, and 7mg sodium. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a carbohydrate-dominant sugar-water beverage with negligible macronutrient diversity. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its 7.0g sugar/100g content and complete absence of dietary fibre. The 'less sugar' designation does not render it clinically safe for metabolic conditions. Barley itself contains beta-glucan, a soluble fibre with cholesterol-lowering properties, but the packaged ready-to-drink format retains negligible fibre. This drink is commonly consumed as a cooling beverage in hot weather and is widely available in Singapore supermarkets and convenience stores in 250ml to 500ml packaging.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D05010028, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). This is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record with no estimation required.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a sugar-water beverage with no protein ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — the barley extraction process used in commercial RTD beverages removes virtually all fibre. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific packaged lemon barley less-sugar product are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for comparable sugar-sweetened lemon-flavoured beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Ontology codes use closest defensible approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for packaged lemon-flavoured less-sugar barley RTD beverages.
